titleStmt.title: Bani Ashur Harran
SHAWI Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the FWF Austrian Science Fund under the number P 33574

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the SHAWI teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the SHAWI network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the dict prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a entry in the SHAWI glossary.
Private URIs using the rend prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a rendition element in VICAV rendition library.
Private URIs using the assets prefix are pointers to the location of the SHAWI media assets.

spanGrp.span: There were two friends who were both sheikhs. They had become friends. One of them had gone to the east and the other to the west.
And time passed. After some time had passed, one of them came to the other, I mean, as a guest.
When he came to him as a guest, in the morning he started preparing coffee and people started coming and and he was the host.
The one to whom the guest came, his familie’s tent was opposite to his. The tent of this one, the host, it was opposite of his paternal uncle’s family. They had a beautiful daughter.

His friend, the guest, wanted the girl, that is, he fell in love with her. After he had fallen in love with the girl, he and she... In the past, a guest would stay for a month or two in the tent (of the host).
So the girl got up and came to him; to the corner of the tent, near the tent for guests belonging to her cousins.
They were spending time together and having fun until the morning and they slept together – his friend and the daughter of his uncle.
When he woke up and wanted to make coffee, he saw the daughter of his uncle and his friend sleeping together.
So he took his sword and started bashing the tent and cutting (the ropes). “Hey you! Hey you!” (he shouted). People from around started coming (as usual) to drink coffee.
He was cutting (the ropes) on all sides of the tent. He wanted to wake her up so she could escape.
And people started saying, “The sheikh has gone mad!” They grabbed him. That girl recognized the noise and so she ran away and escaped.
After she had escaped and they had caught him, he came down and went back making fire and preparing coffee.
So they drank coffee, he and his friend and that girl, his cousin, the one whom (his friend) desired.
He stayed at his place for a while and then he said, “Brother, I want to go.” So, he prepared provisions for him meaning he also gave him what he needed of food (for the road) and left.

He went to back his family. After he had left for his family time passed. As for (his friend), the one who had a cousin, the fate turned against him and no cattle, no property, no thingy remained to him.
He became poor as he had nothing left at all. His wife said to him, “O man, don’t you have a friend or companion in this world? Don’t you have anyone?”

“Let’s go, let’s go to him, let’s go and ask for livelihood, just to live, we don’t want anything else!” He said, “Yes, in fact, I have a friend in the eastern part and I want to go to him and ask him to see what his response will be.”
He loaded… he had only one she-camel. He packed his tent and set off as he had also lost the status of being a sheikh.
When he arrived to his friend that said, “Welcome.” And he said, “In fact, brother, this is what has happened to me.”
He came to his tent where he got up early in the morning and gave orders to all his tribesmen.
Every tent should provide one tent square and then they will collect for him these tent squares, sew them together and put up a tent for him like his own tent.
In each house, there was a camel and a ewe. The one who ordered the tribesmen around him was the sheikh of the tribe.

They had collected cattle for him to match his (the sheikh’s) own; along with his shepherds and servants. They had pitched up a tent for him. So, this one (the host) used to make the coffee in the morning, but in the evening that one (the guest) prepared the coffee.
Their tents were facing each other.
Facing each other.
Facing each other. And time passed and (his) children played with the children of the sheikh.

The first sheikh was the one who originally belonged to the tribal area. The children played until nightfall in the open field. At night, those children would come and sleep at their mother’s; at (the place of) the one who had come.
One night, the friends were sleeping in the bed and when the sheikh came back from his coffee gathering, lo and behold he found a man sleeping in his bed.
He said, “Aha! My wife is betraying me!” He got up, drew his sword, and cut off his head.
No, no! When she woke up, she said to him “No, you! Oh, you unlucky one, this boy is the so-and-so. He is the son of the sheikh.” He replied “And how was I to know?”
“What should we do? What should we do?” He said, “What should we do? Let’s go and inform the sheikh!”
“Yes, go and tell him!”
He got up, went to him and informed him. He said, “O brother, this is the situation, this is what happened!”

“I found him sleeping in my bed while my children were also with my wife, so I killed him.” He said, “Go, wrap him in his blanket, take him to the place where the children are playing and throw him there.”
This was said by his father
This was said by his father
Yes.
“Throw him in the place where the children are playing and leave him!” So they wrapped him up and threw him in the place where the children were playing.
He was sitting in the morning preparing the coffee
Without telling anyone.
Without telling anyone. People came to have coffee.

As they passed by the place where the children were playing, they saw that the young man was wrapped in a blanket and that he was murdered. They looked at him and discovered that he was the sheikh’s son.
They went to the sheikh to inform him... and they were deliberating among themselves about how to tell it to the sheikh.
This one said, “I see you whispering and muttering, tell me, what’s going on?”
They said, “What we will tell you is bad news. We passed by the place where the children are playing and found the so-and-so killed.”
He said, “Go and bring the one who was killed to me!” They brought him, washed him, wrapped him in a shroud and buried him. Then he said “O tribesmen, each one of you has to bring me a she-camel.
And (I don’t know how many) ewes. Go and give them to my brother, to my friend.” The tribesmen gathered everything he asked for and gave it to him.
After some years had passed and after that one had departed they began talking among themselves to determine which of the friends is more in the right.
Yes, he and his friend.
He and his friend.
When they went to the tribal judge to seek his judgment and one of them said, “One day, I did you many favours.” This was said by the owner of the tribal area.
The other said to him, “But my favours were greater than yours.” He asked him, “How is that?” He replied, “Let’s talk about this matter.”

He said “Once I came and found my friend, the sheikh, sleeping with my uncles daughter. So I acted as if I were mad and started cutting the ropes of the tent like this, so that my cousin would leave his bed.
So that the tribesmen wouldn’t ...
hit him.
Hit him, so that they wouldn’t see her.
I didn’t allow him to be killed.
I didn’t allow him to be killed.
Later I came to him possessing nothing. I came to him and he gave me money. The children were playing and one night I saw someone sleeping in my wife’s bed and thought that my wife was betraying me.
And I killed him then.
By mistake.
Yes, by mistake. This is what happened. Which one is right?” They said, “You are the one who is right.
You, the one who has a female cousin, you saw your honour in his embrace but you didn’t kill him nor were you upset with him.
Yes, you weren’t upset with him.
And you still stayed with him (found for him an excuse).
You found an excuse for him and you gave it to him.
And you gave it to him.” So, the one who had the female cousin became the one who was (more) right.
The one who had the female cousin.
More than...
the father of the boy who was killed.
Before the man, the father of the boy who was killed, more than the one with the boy.
More than the one with the boy.
More than the one with the boy. May you live long!
May God keep you safe!
